overall_summary: The 90-day ai16z to elizaOS token migration window has officially closed, transitioning the community focus toward v2.0 multi-language framework support and global authentication hardening. Technical efforts are currently centered on cloud credit administration and resolving runtime errors in the bootstrap plugin.
key_facts: The ai16z to elizaOS token migration deadline closed after a 90-day window with a 1:6 conversion ratio.
PR #6200 implemented a comprehensive JWT authentication system to enable data isolation and multi-entity support.
The core team consolidated n8n plugin efforts around the plugin-n8n-workflow repository due to its superior feature set.
ElizaOS v2.0 development is introducing native support for Rust and Python alongside TypeScript in the core framework.
Cloud platform credits are being managed manually via UPDATE operations in the user database for accounts created through OAuth providers.
A Moltbook verification solver was successfully implemented using the DeepSeek-chat model to handle math-based anti-bot challenges.
PR #6475 introduced the ActionFilterService using vector search and BM25 reranking to reduce prompt bloat.
The Babylon project clarified that its non-chain launch is imminent, while the token launch is scheduled months away.
open_questions: How do we stop the bleeding in token price performance?
Are there any plans for the Eliza NFT collection (formerly ai16z partner)?
Will the ai16z Singularities collection have a use in the new ecosystem?
Is it possible to change the Singularities collection name from ai16z?
Will both NFT collections be included in the ecosystem in the future?
Does anyone here have a project idea or need a developer?

new_issues_prs.title: feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Expands framework accessibility to Rust and Python developers while removing default infra for a more modular approach.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(auth): implement JWT authentication and user management
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Crucial security milestone for multi-tenant data isolation and external JWT provider integration.

new_issues_prs.title: fix: Add null/undefined checks to prevent Object.entries errors in plugin-bootstrap
new_issues_prs.author: anchapin
new_issues_prs.significance: Prevents critical runtime crashes in the bootstrap provider when values are null.

new_issues_prs.title: [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls
new_issues_prs.author: thewoweffect
new_issues_prs.significance: Identifies an inefficiency where URLs are processed as both text and attachments, doubling token costs.

overall_focus.claim: The focus has shifted toward architectural modularity (v2.0) and enterprise security hardening (JWT, transaction guardrails).

discord_updates.summary: Focus on cloud administration and database-level credit management. Resolved issues finding OAuth-based accounts using organization slugs.
Clarification of the token migration deadline. Support tickets are only being processed for those who applied before the 90-day cutoff.
Debate over wallet management architectures, with a preference for HD wallets over heavy Privy-based OAuth approaches.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed frustration over the closed migration window and the lack of a clear timeline for zero-coding agent creation.

strategic_insights.insight: Community consensus suggests ElizaOS's main advantage over centralized AI like ChatGPT is decentralized data ownership and modular web3-native integration.
implications_or_questions: Does this focus on decentralization sufficiently attract non-technical users?

strategic_insights.insight: Duplicate efforts in plugin development (like n8n) hinder standard adoption; consolidation is required to maintain quality parity.
implications_or_questions: How can the registry better enforce single-purpose modules?

market_analysis.observation: Market speculation regarding 'pippin' token performance despite creator inactivity on social media.
market_analysis.relevance: Highlights a disconnect between social hype and tokenomics/performance.
